elements.title: forty-hadith
elements.contents: ... that works in the forty-hadith genre (example pictured) were produced based on a hadith promising special treatment on Judgement Day?

elements.title: Awlad al-Imam
elements.contents: ... that the Awlad al-Imam were venerated for their knowledge and legacy, but none of their works have survived?

elements.title: Canal of Zubaidah
elements.contents: ... that the Canal of Zubaidah, built on the order of a queen, provided water to Hajj pilgrims for over a thousand years?

elements.title: Intisar A. Rabb
elements.contents: ... that American academic Intisar A. Rabb was inspired by a Supreme Court blog to launch an online portal containing Sharia texts?

elements.title: Para Perintis Kemerdekaan
elements.contents: ... that one scholar read the film Para Perintis Kemerdekaan as offering a "revolutionary Islam" that seeks to emancipate women?

elements.title: Japan's most renowned Islamic scholar
elements.contents: ... that "Japan's most renowned Islamic scholar" once wrote a light novel and a manga?

elements.title: Shukr
elements.contents: ... that the Jordan-based fashion company Shukr sells clothing named after prominent Muslim women?

elements.title: a sweet dish resembling a bird's nest
elements.contents: ... that people in the Indian subcontinent eat a sweet dish resembling a bird's nest during Ramadan?

elements.title: Raşit Küçük
elements.contents: ... that Raşit Küçük, a biographer of Muhammad, earned his doctorate exploring love?
